title: Green Lake Park - Seattle.gov
content: # Green Lake Park. 7201 E Green Lake Dr. N, Seattle, WA 98115. ## Green Lake Park Attractions. people in an outdoor swimming pool. Children in the Green Lake Park wading pool. Evans Pool at Green Lake, viewed from behind a starting block at the deep end. Table tennis in the Green Lake Community Center gymnasium. Green Lake Park in Spring. Green Lake is one of Seattle's most beloved parks. Its expanse of water and green space in the center of a dense urban neighborhood draws thousands of people daily from all over the city. Many others use the athletic fields or visit the park for boating, picnics, and swimming. The cove is 50' of shoreline at the eastern corner of Green Lake, facing north, south of the East Green Lake Beach. Green Lake Park has two lifeguarded swimming areas:. * East Green Lake Beach at 7201 East Green Lake Dr. N., near the Green Lake Community Center and Evans Pool.

title: Green Lake Park — Park Review | Condé Nast Traveler
content: A walk around which will introduce anyone to the staples of Seattle culture. More than a century later, the crowds walking or rolling the three-mile path around the lake rarely subside, the sports fields and facilities bring people from all over the city, and the beaches attract crowds throughout the summer. A walk around Green Lake introduces anyone to staples of Seattle culture, mostly through the people one passes: the walkers, the fishermen, the picnicking families, and the soccer players. They key to finding your way around Green Lake is starting anywhere: it can be difficult to coordinate locations when there are two parks, two beaches, a boathouse, a bathhouse that's now a theater, and a theater that's now a boathouse. Green Lake requires about an hour to properly circle, longer if you plan to stop for a swim or to rent a human-powered boat or paddleboard and get out onto the lake.

title: Green Lake Seattle | Things To Do, The Loop Trail And Local Dining
content: # Spending a Day in Seattle’s Green Lake Neighborhood. Things To Do | Coffee | Food and Drink | King County | Seattle. One of my favorite aspects of living in the Seattle area is all of the different experiences you are surrounded with. The Green Lake neighborhood is just north of downtown Seattle. A quick 15-minute drive will take you away from the high rises and bring you to this low-key neighborhood built around the lake it’s named after. If you’re looking for a way to spend your day, here are some of the many attractions of the Green Lake neighborhood. If you’re in Seattle and don’t **start your day with coffee**, are you even in Seattle? While there’s a place to get coffee on every corner in this city, I personally recommend stopping by **Retreat** to fuel up for your adventure. ## Spend Some Time in Green Lake Park. Green Lake Pitch & Putt is a great way to spend some time after you complete your walk around the lake.
